title: oxalis viscosa
intro: Oxalis viscosa, also known as sticky wood sorrel, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. Native to South America, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The plant's leaves and stems are rich in oxalic acid, which has been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, making it a potential natural remedy for various ailments. Oxalis viscosa can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its herbal preparations have been used to treat conditions such as wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
